summary: Fair matrix recording with decent frequency range but unbalanced levels; show is mixed but highlights a stellar Watchtower, Brent‑driven Playing in the Band and a short yet solid I Need a Miracle.
review: The UltraMatrix SBD provides a fairly clear picture of the concert, offering more frequencies than other sources though it suffers from hum and uneven leveling. Musically the performance is uneven – the first set dips after a strong start and the second set has highs like an energized All Along the Watchtower and a Brent‑powered Playing in the Band, but also weaker moments on Scarlet Begonias and Not Fade Away.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review gives an overall 3¼‑star rating; the show has both strong highlights and noticeable flaws, while the recording is usable but not perfectly balanced.

recording_quality.technical_notes: UltraMatrix SBD captures a wider frequency range than other sources, has a mild hum, and lacks proper balancing/leveling; other available mixes have glitches and low volume.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts solid, dips around High Time and recovers, while the second set is uneven – strong moments (Watchtower, Playing in the Band, Miracle) interspersed with weaker jams (Scarlet verses, lackluster Drums>Space). Overall a mixed‑quality performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Appears spent later in the show; less energetic compared to Bob during Walkin' Blues.
band_member_comments.Bob: Guitar work strong, especially on Watchtower and generally better than Jerry in the early blues numbers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) is pivotal on Playing in the Band, delivering an ace performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drums>Space segment isn’t as compelling as elsewhere on this tour.
